refinement_sections.content: I need to analyze how different CPU scheduling algorithms perform on a new multi-core processor. I should find out which one is best for the workloads at Tech Innovators Corp.
I have to choose at least three scheduling algorithms to compare. Some common ones might be First-Come, First-Served (FCFS), Round Robin (RR), and Shortest Job First (SJF).

I need to select workloads that reflect real-world usage. This could mean looking at different types of applications like gaming, web browsing, and data processing. I should probably choose a mix of CPU-bound and I/O-bound tasks.

I need to measure response time, throughput, and fairness. Response time is how fast the system responds, throughput is how many tasks are completed in a certain time, and fairness means how evenly the CPU time is distributed among all tasks.
I will set up an experiment where I run each scheduling algorithm with the chosen workloads. I will collect data on the evaluation metrics and then compare the results.
I will assume that the workloads I select are representative of what users actually do, and that the multi-core system works properly without any major faults during testing.

After I finish the experiment, I need to write a report summarizing what I found. I will include my methodology, the workloads I used, the results of my tests, and my recommendations on which scheduling algorithm is best.
